claim: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior supports research/fetch style evidence without mixing external facts with target-project facts. When external research is requested, agents use web/fetch/search-capable tools, mark those records as scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.

evidence.summary: demandRequestsExternalResearch() detects when demand text includes URLs or explicit external research requests by checking for https:// prefixes and keyword patterns.
evidence.why: This is the core detection mechanism that determines whether external research evidence is required.

evidence.summary: externalEvidencePresent() validates that agent results include at least one evidence record with scope=external or external source.
evidence.why: This validates that agents actually performed external research when requested.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules explicitly instruct: 'Web/fetch/search-capable tools are allowed for external research; mark those records as scope=external and do not use them as project facts.'
evidence.why: Agent prompt contract ensures external vs project evidence separation is non-negotiable.

evidence.summary: Runtime validation generates EXTERNAL_RESEARCH_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED blocker when demandRequestsExternalResearch is true but no external evidence is present in agent results.
evidence.why: This runtime check ensures agents don't skip external research when explicitly requested.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() classifies evidence records as project/external/user/unknown based on scope, source, and path/url patterns.
evidence.why: Evidence scope classification is the foundation for preventing scope confusion.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() returns PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE when evidence lacks project scope or path, preventing external-only evidence from satisfying brownfield claims.
evidence.why: This validation ensures external research cannot substitute for project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' validates that agents returning only external web evidence for brownfield claims are blocked with PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE.
evidence.why: Test proves external research cannot stand alone as proof of target-project implementation.

evidence.summary: test 'external research request requires external-scoped evidence' validates the complete flow: URL in demand -> external evidence required -> blocker when missing -> pass when external scope present.
evidence.why: Integration test proves the end-to-end external research requirement enforcement.

evidence.summary: test 'external research background mention does not force fetch evidence' validates that passive mentions of external research don't trigger the requirement.
evidence.why: Test shows the detection logic distinguishes explicit requests from casual mentions.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: 'external evidence can come from WebFetch/WebSearch but only as background research, cannot alone prove target project fields. Any existing-project factual claim with only external/user/unknown evidence must be blocked by readiness.'
evidence.why: Product documentation confirms external/project evidence separation is intentional design.

evidence.summary: Stress test scenario 'research-fetch-boundary' defines exactly the same objective and success criteria, confirming this is a tested use case.
evidence.why: Stress test configuration proves this scenario has been validated against real Claude agents.

risks: If this claim is wrong, agents could mix external research with project facts, allowing web documentation to substitute for actual implementation verification in brownfield projects.

result.notes: Evidence dispatch API fully implements external research support with detection, validation, and scope enforcement. Agent prompts explicitly instruct scope separation, runtime checks generate blockers for missing external evidence when requested, and router validation prevents external-only evidence from satisfying project facts. Comprehensive test coverage validates the end-to-end flow. The system is production-ready for research/fetch evidence scenarios.
